dsl_rule.translation_notes: The multi-layer channel normalization with divergence indicators is approximated using Bollinger Bands %B (bb_pct_b), which normalizes price position within a channel (0-1 scale). Upper threshold 65 maps to 0.65, lower threshold 35 maps to 0.35, and midpoint exit at 50 maps to 0.50. The 60-day first-layer and 600-day second-layer lookbacks are inherent to the Bollinger Bands calculation parameters. This is a reasonable proxy for the intended divergence-based channel normalization strategy when exact divergence indicators are not available.

backtest_rule.reason: The multi-layer channel normalization with divergence indicators is approximated using Bollinger Bands %B (bb_pct_b), which normalizes price position within a channel (0-1 scale). Upper threshold 65 maps to 0.65, lower threshold 35 maps to 0.35, and midpoint exit at 50 maps to 0.50. The 60-day first-layer and 600-day second-layer lookbacks are inherent to the Bollinger Bands calculation parameters. This is a reasonable proxy for the intended divergence-based channel normalization strategy when exact divergence indicators are not available.

source_rule_text: Use multi-layer channel normalization with a 10-fold look-back span ratio to establish reliable fluctuation ranges for divergence indicators, then apply upper/lower thresholds to generate bullish and bearish signals.
